First time visit here....we rode first on the iconic Wonder Wheel Ferris wheel, which started operation in 1920!  So much history here.

After riding the subway from New York City for 60 minutes, we took our 18 and 22 year-old to Coney Island, thinking it would be a nice evening for them to ride some rides. We walked from one side of  the park to the opposite side to figure out which ones they wanted to ride.

The kids chose three different rides and we walked up to a machine that you could purchase a card with tickets. We purchased $60 worth of tickets and took the kids to ride the Phoenix roller coaster. 

When they were done we moved over to steeplechase, only to be told that the card was only valid for Dino’s wonder wheel and that it didn’t include any of the other rides that are “in Luna‘s park” it turns out that 90% of the park is considered Luna Park and some stupid strip with a small number of rides in between  is considered Dino’s wonder wheel, which lies in between parks within Luna Park. Very disappointing.
